title: Understanding Web Authentication: JWT and OAuth

questions.sentence: In modern web applications, secure user authentication is paramount.
A JWT is a compact, self-contained way for securely transmitting information between parties as a JSON object.
The browser stores this token (usually in local storage or a cookie) and includes it in the header of future API requests to verify the user's identity.

OAuth, on the other hand, is an open-standard authorization protocol that allows users to grant third-party applications access to their information without sharing passwords—such as 'Login with Google'.
While JWT handles authentication (who you are), OAuth is primarily about authorization (what you can do).
